steps.title: Identify what property the corpus measures
steps.content: The passage explains that corpora empirically test "how ______ a word is" using its incidence — its frequency in spoken and written English. The example demonstrates this by proving "first" is the "third most commonly used adjective," confirming the blank refers to how common a word is.

steps.title: Match frequency to the correct choice
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean how widespread or frequently occurring a word is — incidence and "most commonly used" are the key clues. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — pervasive: means widespread or prevalent throughout — directly captures the concept of frequency and incidence in language. ✓
  • Choice B — credible: means believable or trustworthy — describes reliability of a source or claim, not how often a word appears. ✗
  • Choice C — assertive: means confidently forceful — describes a communicative style or tone, not word frequency. ✗
  • Choice D — profound: means deeply meaningful or significant — describes conceptual depth, not how commonly a word occurs in a corpus. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
